title: crucianella maritima subsp. rupestris
intro: Crucianella maritima subsp. rupestris, also known as Rock Broom or Rock Crucianell, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its expectorant, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and ease muscle spasms. The health benefits of Crucianella maritima subsp. rupestris include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
